summary: Above‑average audience tape with strong performances – Jerry’s guitar shines and The Other One jam stands out despite occasional wobble in the sound.
review: The show is remembered for its high energy, solid renditions of Good Lovin', Casey Jones, Hard to Handle and a memorable The Other One jam, plus an uplifting Uncle John’s Band encore.  Most reviewers praise the musical vibe and Jerry Garcia’s playing, while noting that the audience recording has typical 1970 tape hiss, occasional crackle and balance quirks that were later smoothed in a remaster.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the performance 4‑5 stars and cite standout songs, while the audio is described as good to above‑average for an audience tape; a few harsh negatives lower the overall average but do not dominate.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Typical 1970 audience tape – audible hiss, occasional wobble/crackle and balance issues that were later remastered; some listeners call it “pretty good for aud” while a few label it “horrible”.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy electric set (part of an acoustic/NRPS/electric combo night) with strong mid‑set jams and a crowd‑engaging encore; the band kept the momentum up throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note Jerry “kicks butt”, his tone is perfect, and he drives the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Casey Jones highlighted for its tight drumming and polyrhythms toward the end.
